excerpt: The nafs ʿāmira bil-suʾ (the self inciting to evil) draws the soul toward transgression
source_location_hint: on the classification of souls

excerpt: The term ʿaqr (hamstringing) and dhbīḥa (ritual slaughter) refer to lawful death, yet the inner meaning refers to the souls who perpetrated evil
source_location_hint: on the slaughter of the self

excerpt: The muqāṣir endures the torment of the grave for three days, after which the body is subjected to punishment commensurate with human stature
source_location_hint: on the grave torment of those who fall short

excerpt: Allah forgives sins against Himself but not against His servants, except through the means by which justice is fulfilled between them
source_location_hint: on divine forgiveness and retribution

excerpt: Allah rewards the good deeds of the righteous even if they have not received a good ending; evil has no origin in creation
source_location_hint: on the reward of good deeds

excerpt: The self of the body is progress; its diffusion into the body is degradation. What Allah creates cannot be destroyed; only form changes while existence remains
source_location_hint: on the nature of soul and body

excerpt: While joined to the body, the devil is in potentiality (shayṭān bil-quwwa); separated, it becomes a devil in actuality (shayṭān bil-fiʿl)
source_location_hint: on the states of the devil

excerpt: Those whose evil forms become Muslim become praiseworthy jinns (jinn maḥmūd), while those who persist in disobedience remain condemned jinns (jinn manzūm)
source_location_hint: on the types of jinn

excerpt: Evil ingredients are absorbed into food and drink by the will of the wise man, yet they have no effect upon the saints but come to light through them
source_location_hint: on the manifestation of evil forms through food

excerpt: The poisoned herb revealed itself because the herbal component outranked the poison in station and revealed its true condition
source_location_hint: on the incident of the poisoned herb

excerpt: The Imam of the age is a bringer of good tidings for his contemporaries and those who acknowledge him, yet he becomes a denier for those who reject him
source_location_hint: on the dual nature of the Imam

excerpt: Evil deeds become beasts that frighten and hurt the evildoer; they are the companion of his actions
source_location_hint: on the personification of deeds

excerpt: If believers commit similar sins, they too are punished but need not don seventy garments; nature punishes them according to justice and returns them to human stature
source_location_hint: on the differential punishment of believers and non-believers

excerpt: The cold sphere (kurat al-zamharrīr) produces the cold of iron (barad al-ḥadīd), a punishment for the multitude of Imams
source_location_hint: on the northern torment

excerpt: Those separated from the grasp of Allah's saints walk in darkness toward three-split shadows, which are the torments of Dārkāt, Hafdār, and Saqāra
source_location_hint: on the three shadows of torment

excerpt: After the extremal torments, souls appear in the inhabited quarter and ascend the staircase through minerals, plants, and animals until they appear before the Qāʾim
source_location_hint: on the stages of return from torment

excerpt: All the Ḥudūd, Prophets, and Imāms will gather in the presence of the Qāʾim...the Qāʾim will slay them all. A fire descends from them which consumes their defiled forms; these ascend as vapor and are absorbed within it.
source_location_hint: eschatological teaching on the fate of opposers

excerpt: Saqqara lies at the earth's nadir. The fever penetrates the interior of the sacred realm through its pores...This is what the Qur'ān refers to as the record of the wicked (sījīn).
source_location_hint: cosmological description of Saqqara

excerpt: The scorching fuel beneath Saqqara possesses a heat equivalent to that of the sun itself. As fire heats a pot from below, so too does the sun heat Suqra from beneath, causing the material to ignite.
source_location_hint: thermological metaphor for infernal heat

excerpt: In the days of Saqqara's people, extreme heat by day and bitter cold by night—beyond reckoning—assail them. When both affect them in succession, punishment increases a hundredfold.
source_location_hint: doctrine of graduated torment

excerpt: The center of the entire universe is the Sun, which orbits the Earth...All celestial bodies orient themselves toward the earth so that humanity—the extraction of humanity—may emerge here.
source_location_hint: cosmological and anthropological doctrine

excerpt: Saturn and Mars were commanded that they would remain with you, and that their purification would be your burden. Consequently, their effects deteriorate.
source_location_hint: astral influences on moral purification

excerpt: They shall slay one another and slay themselves yet shall not perish. They die and fall, and instantly revive; in this manner their narratives are fulfilled. This punishment persists for an age of darkness.
source_location_hint: perpetual cycle of torment and resurrection

excerpt: When their sentence concludes, they are resurrected in human stature, and the punishment they have endured is forgotten. They are then invited...This divine mill continues its rotation in such wise.
source_location_hint: doctrine of repeated opportunity and divine mercy

excerpt: The Angels of Wrath, at the behest of the Tenth, in the holy world and natural world, drive away those of malevolent forms. For of these matters the administrator and sovereign are the disposers. He arranges all things in their rank by the trumpets.
source_work: Rasāʾil Ikhwān al-Ṣafāʾ (as cited in KASHKOLE)
source_location_hint: angelic hierarchies and cosmic administration
